ι : Type u_1
Ω : Type u_2
E : Type u_3
inst✝⁴ : LinearOrder ι
inst✝³ : TopologicalSpace ι
inst✝² : NormedAddCommGroup E
inst✝¹ : NormedSpace ℝ E
inst✝ : CompleteSpace E
mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω
P : Measure Ω
X Y : ι → Ω → E
𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ
hX : @ProbabilityTheory.IsSquareIntegrable ι Ω E inst✝⁴ inst✝³ inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ mΩ X 𝓕 P
hY : @ProbabilityTheory.IsSquareIntegrable ι Ω E inst✝⁴ inst✝³ inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ mΩ Y 𝓕 P
hX_bound : ⨆ i, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P < ⊤
hY_bound : ⨆ i, eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P < ⊤
⊢ ⨆ i, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P + eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P ≤ (⨆ i, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P) + ⨆ i, eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P

                simp [iSup_add_right]

          -- Apply the property of the supremum for sums
          calc
            ⨆ i : ι, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P + eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P ≤ ⨆ i : ι, (eLpNorm (X i) 2 P + eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P) := le_iSup (fun i : ι => eLpNorm (X i) 2 P + eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P)
            _ = ⨆ i : ι, (eLpNorm (X i) 2 P + eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P) := rfl
            _ ≤ (⨆ i : ι, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P) + ⨆ i : ι, eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P := by
              -- Use the subadditivity of the supremum
              apply iSup_add_le
              <;>
                (try simp_all) <;>
                (try norm_num) <;>
                (try linarith) <;>
                (try
                  {
                    apply le_of_not_gt
                    intro h₂
                    refine' h₂.ne' (by simp_all [hX_bound, hY_bound])
                  })
              <;>
                (try
                  {
                    simp_all [hX_bound, hY_bound]
                    <;>
                    norm_num at *
                    <;>
                    linarith
                  })
        -- Use the obtained inequality
        calc
          ⨆ i, (eLpNorm (X i) 2 P + eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P) = ⨆ i : ι, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P + eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P := rfl
          _ ≤ (⨆ i : ι, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P) + ⨆ i : ι, eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P := h
          _ = (⨆ i, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P) + ⨆ i, eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P := rfl
      _ < ∞ := by
        -- Prove that the sum is finite
        have h₁ : (⨆ i, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P : ℝ≥0∞) < ∞ := by simpa using hX_bound
        have h₂ : (⨆ i, eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P : ℝ≥0∞) < ∞ := by simpa using hY_bound
        -- Use the fact that the sum of finite numbers is finite
        have h₃ : (⨆ i, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P : ℝ≥0∞) + (⨆ i, eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P : ℝ≥0∞) < ∞ := by

          norm_num

          linarith
        -- Use the fact that the sum of finite numbers is finite
